title: The 3D Kinetic Couette Flow Via The Boltzmann Equation In The Diffusive Limit
speaker: Robert Strain
abstract:
This talk is about the study of the Boltzmann equation in the diffusive limit in a channel domain 𝕋2×(−1,1)
 nearby the 3D kinetic Couette flow.  We will begin the talk with a substantial introduction for non-experts.  Our result demonstrates that the first-order approximation of a solution to the Boltzmann equation is governed by the perturbed incompressible Navier-Stokes-Fourier system around the fluid Couette flow.  Moverover, in the absence of external forces, we show that the 3D kinetic Couette flow  converges in large time to the 1D steady planar kinetic Couette flow.  Our method relies on three ingredients: (i) the Fourier transform on 𝕋2 allows us to reduce the 3D problem to 1D, (ii) anisotropic Chemin-Lerner type function spaces (using the Wiener algebra) are used to bound the nonlinear terms and to control the singularity associated with a small Knudsen number in the diffusive limit, and (iii) the Caflisch decomposition, combined with the L2∩L∞
 technique, to manage the growth of large velocities.  This is a joint work in collaboration with Renjun Duan, Shuangqian Liu, and Anita Yang.

proof_stages.label: Kinetic Couette model and boundary/collision structure
proof_stages.summary: The talk formulates three-dimensional Couette flow in a periodic channel, writes the rescaled Boltzmann equation with Knudsen number and diffuse moving-wall boundary conditions, and specifies the angular-cutoff hard-potential collision operator.

proof_stages.label: Peculiar-velocity reduction and stationary target problem
proof_stages.summary: Passing to velocity relative to the macroscopic shear transforms the moving-wall problem into an equation with shear transport terms and a global Maxwellian boundary condition, leading to a non-explicit steady Boltzmann problem with a prescribed mass.

proof_stages.label: Linearization and hydrodynamic projection
proof_stages.summary: Linearizing around the Maxwellian produces the linearized collision operator, whose five-dimensional velocity null space defines the macroscopic density, velocity, and temperature variables used in the fluid limit.

proof_stages.label: Hilbert expansions and fluid equations
proof_stages.summary: The steady and time-dependent solutions are expanded in powers of the Knudsen number, yielding coefficient equations in which the leading macroscopic fields satisfy the incompressible Navier--Stokes--Fourier system around Couette flow and leaving explicit remainder equations with diffuse boundary terms.

proof_stages.label: Wiener-algebra and kinetic remainder estimates
proof_stages.summary: The proof uses horizontal Fourier analysis, anisotropic Chemin--Lerner/Wiener algebra norms, Caflisch splitting, and L2--L∞ kinetic estimates to control nonlinear terms, large velocities, boundary effects, and the singular small-Knudsen scaling uniformly.

proof_stages.label: Stationary existence and diffusive-limit theorem
proof_stages.summary: Under small shear and force assumptions the stationary boundary value problem has a unique nonnegative strong solution with a Hilbert expansion, uniformly bounded remainders, and an ε→0 limit that derives the corresponding Navier--Stokes--Fourier Couette system.

proof_stages.label: Dynamic stability and exponential convergence
proof_stages.summary: For small compatible perturbations of the stationary kinetic Couette profile, the time-dependent Boltzmann problem has a unique nonnegative solution whose remainder decays exponentially, giving convergence to the planar kinetic Couette flow uniformly for small Knudsen number.

examples.note: Pictures of mirror-like and diffuse optical reflection make the diffuse-reflection boundary condition visually concrete, without contributing to the proof.
